title: Revalidate auth on long-lived connections
description: Authorization is re-checked as tokens expire or permissions change, not trusted from the initial handshake forever.
body: Use when a connection outlives its access token or a user's permissions can change mid-session. Do not authorize only at the WebSocket/SSE handshake and then stream indefinitely. Actions: track token expiry per connection, require a refresh/re-auth message before expiry, re-check channel authorization on resubscribe, and close or downgrade the connection when the token is revoked or expires. Validation signals include a test that a connection whose token expires stops receiving authorized data until it re-auths and that revocation severs the stream. Risk boundary: a connection that streams private data on an expired or revoked token is a security finding requiring a human gate.
